title: Cassine humbertii
intro: Cassine humbertii, also known as "bois d'arc" or "sacred cedar", is a medicinal plant species native to Madagascar, characterized by its distinctive, aromatic wood and resinous properties. The health benefits of Cassine humbertii include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cassine humbertii include triterpenes, flavonoids, and essential oils, particularly cedrol and cedrene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Cassine humbertii include essential oils, infusions, and decoctions, often used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.

Cassine humbertii is one of the lesser-known species in this genus, and more research is needed to fully understand its classification and characteristics.
